verdicts.title: Reflected XSS via unescaped REQUEST_URI in admin notice href (dismiss link)
verdicts.description: In wccp_free_review_notice_message(), $_SERVER['REQUEST_URI'] is concatenated with a query string suffix and passed through wp_nonce_url(). The resulting $dismiss_url is echoed directly into an href attribute without esc_url() sanitization. wp_nonce_url() only appends the _wpnonce parameter to the URL; it does not HTML-encode the URL for safe output in an href context. An attacker who induces an admin to visit a crafted admin URL can inject arbitrary content into the href attribute, enabling JavaScript execution.
taint_chain: Line 123: $scheme derived from wp_parse_url($_SERVER['REQUEST_URI']) — taint source
Line 125: $dismiss_url = wp_nonce_url($url, 'wccp_free_review-nonce') — taint propagation (wp_nonce_url does not esc_url)
Line 144: echo $dismiss_url — raw echo in href= attribute context — SINK
verdicts.exploit_scenario: An attacker crafts a URL pointing to any admin page but with a REQUEST_URI containing a JavaScript URI or attribute-breaking sequence, e.g.: /wp-admin/index.php/%22%20onmouseover=%22alert(document.cookie). When the authenticated admin browses this URL and the review notice banner is displayed (after 5 days of activation), the injected content appears inside the href attribute, enabling XSS. Because this is an admin-context admin_notices hook, the attacker must socially engineer the admin to click the crafted link.
exploitation_conditions: Plugin must have been active for >5 days (432000 seconds) OR development_mode=true
Review notice must not have been previously dismissed
Authenticated admin must follow a crafted URL
verdicts.false_positive_analysis: NOT a false positive. wp_nonce_url() wraps add_query_arg() which does not HTML-encode the URL. The output at line 144 is a raw echo with no esc_url() wrapper. The href context is exploitable via attribute injection or javascript: protocol.
verdicts.fix: Wrap all URL outputs in esc_url(): echo esc_url($dismiss_url) and echo esc_url($later_url). Also consider sanitizing $_SERVER['REQUEST_URI'] with esc_url() before use.

verdicts.title: Reflected XSS via unescaped REQUEST_URI in admin notice href (later link)
verdicts.description: Identical to xss-001 but for the $later_url variable built from $_SERVER['REQUEST_URI']. At line 127: $_later_link = $_SERVER['REQUEST_URI'] . $scheme . 'wccp_free_review_later=yes'; line 128: $later_url = wp_nonce_url($_later_link, 'wccp_free_review-nonce'); line 145: echo $later_url — unescaped in href context.
taint_chain: Line 123: $scheme derived from wp_parse_url($_SERVER['REQUEST_URI']) — taint source
Line 145: echo $later_url — raw echo in href= attribute context — SINK
verdicts.exploit_scenario: Same as xss-001. Appears in the 'Will Rate Later' link of the review notice banner.
verdicts.false_positive_analysis: NOT a false positive. Same root cause as xss-001.
verdicts.dedup_note: Same root cause as xss-001; both share the same line 123 taint source and same missing esc_url() fix.

verdicts.title: Reflected XSS via unescaped REQUEST_URI in admin notice href (dismiss link, second instance)
verdicts.description: A third instance of the same $dismiss_url echo appears at line 146 for the 'Hide Forever' link. Shares the exact same taint flow as xss-001.
taint_chain: Line 123: $scheme derived from wp_parse_url($_SERVER['REQUEST_URI']) — taint source
Line 146: echo $dismiss_url — raw echo in href= attribute — SINK
verdicts.false_positive_analysis: NOT a false positive. Same root cause as xss-001.
verdicts.fix: Use echo esc_url($dismiss_url). Note: xss-001 and xss-003 are the same variable echoed twice; fixing esc_url($dismiss_url) in one place covers both.
verdicts.dedup_note: Same $dismiss_url as xss-001; differs only in which link element it appears in.

additional_findings.description: echo $wccp_settings['smessage'] at preventer-index.php:292 (inside div in admin footer). smessage is sanitized on save via wccp_sanitize($val, 'textbox') which applies stripslashes(htmlentities($val, ENT_QUOTES)). HTML entities are stored in DB and output renders as escaped literal text. Not exploitable.

echo $wccp_settings['prnt_scr_msg'] at preventer-index.php:320 inside CSS content property. prnt_scr_msg is sanitized on save via wccp_sanitize($val, 'text') = stripslashes(htmlentities($val, ENT_QUOTES)). The double-quote character is encoded as " which cannot terminate the CSS string quote, preventing CSS injection.
echo $admin_email_wccp at admin-core.php:140. Value comes from get_bloginfo('admin_email') which is the site admin email address — trusted WP option, not user-controlled taint from HTTP request.

notes: Plugin has very limited attack surface (no AJAX handlers, REST endpoints, or file uploads). The three confirmed findings all share one root cause: missing esc_url() on $_SERVER['REQUEST_URI']-derived URLs echoed into href attributes in the admin review notice. All require admin authentication and activation for 5+ days. Priority score of 3 (low) is appropriate; real-world exploitability is constrained by the social engineering requirement and admin-only scope.
